QR GeneratorWiFi QRLogo QRFree QRCompareFAQ

No Internet? No Problem.

Load the QR tool once while you have a connection. Disconnect. Generate codes offline. Every QR code is created right in your browser — no server required, not even for the first load.

How to Use QR Codes Offline

  1. Visit the homepage while connected to the internet
  2. Let the page fully load (all assets are cached by your browser)
  3. Disconnect from the internet (airplane mode works)
  4. Generate QR codes normally — everything runs locally
  5. Download your QR codes as PNG or SVG

A PWA version with full offline support is coming soon.

Offline QR: Comparison

Tool Offline? Install Sign-up Limits
QRMint (this) Yes ✓ None (open in browser) None ✓ None
Bitly No None (web app) Required 500/mo free
QR Code Monkey No None (web app) Not required No daily limit
Adobe Express QR No None (web app) Required Branded QR (paid)

Good For

🏭 Factory floors
Generate labels for parts and equipment where there is no Wi-Fi or cellular signal.
🎪 Trade shows & events
Create badges, booth signs, and handout codes on-site without relying on venue internet.
🌍 Remote areas
Field workers in areas with spotty coverage can still generate and print codes.
🔒 Secure environments
Air-gapped machines and classified areas where internet access is restricted.

FAQ

Does the page really work without internet?

Yes. Once the page loads, the QR code generation library (qrcode.js) runs entirely in your browser. No AJAX calls, no API requests, no server-side rendering. You can disconnect immediately after the first load.

Can I install this as an app?

We are working on a PWA (Progressive Web App) version that will support full offline install. For now, bookmark the page and it will work from cache after the first visit.

Will the download feature work offline?

Yes. PNG and SVG downloads use browser-native APIs (canvas.toBlob and Blob URLs) that work offline. No server needed.

© 2026 QRMint — free QR code generator. Static codes never expire.
